examples.title: Basic Object Pointer Declaration and Access
examples.description: Shows how to declare object pointers and access members using arrow operator
examples.explanation: Object pointer 'ptr' holds address of 'obj'. Arrow operator (->) provides direct access to object's members without dereferencing.

examples.title: Dynamic Object Creation with new and delete
examples.description: Demonstrates heap allocation and deallocation for objects
examples.explanation: new allocates memory and calls constructor. delete calls destructor first then frees memory. Setting pointer to nullptr prevents dangling pointer.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) where n is name length

examples.title: this Pointer Demonstration
examples.description: Shows usage of this pointer to distinguish member variables
examples.explanation: this is automatically passed to member functions. Used to access current object's members and to return current object pointer.

examples.title: Polymorphism with Base Class Pointer
examples.description: Demonstrates runtime polymorphism using base pointer to derived objects
examples.explanation: Base class pointer can hold addresses of derived objects. Virtual functions enable runtime binding - correct function called based on actual object type.

examples.title: Array of Object Pointers
examples.description: Shows how to create and use array of pointers to objects
examples.explanation: Array of pointers stores addresses of separately allocated objects. Each element accessed via emp[i]->member().
